title: Thinking Machines Lab launches Inkling, its first open-source model
summary: The startup bets on specialized models and presents Inkling, an open-weight model that competes with generalist alternatives.

title: NVIDIA and SEGA resume their historic partnership to bring games to RTX Spark
summary: The collaboration will allow titles like Virtua Fighter Crossroads to be compatible with the RTX Spark platform starting next year.

title: xAI publishes Grok-Build code on GitHub after a data breach
summary: xAI's AI tool becomes open source after a security breach was detected.

title: Steam Machine with Windows 11 offers performance similar to SteamOS
summary: Comparative tests show that the performance of the Steam Machine with Windows 11 is very close to that offered by SteamOS.

title: RPCS3 emulator can now run 75% of PlayStation 3 games on PC
summary: The emulation project reaches a new milestone by making the majority of PS3 titles playable on computers.

title: CXMT's DDR5 memory does not match SK Hynix but Chinese company emerges as second largest DRAM producer
summary: Initial tests reveal performance and overclocking limitations, while the company approaches Micron in production capacity.

title: OnePlus plans to withdraw from US and European markets
summary: The Chinese phone manufacturer would be preparing to cease operations in the West, according to multiple reports.

title: Spotify expands supervised accounts for children to its free plan and more countries
summary: The streaming platform extends the functionality of parent-managed accounts to users without a paid subscription.

title: SpaceX launches Starlink V5 residential kit, smaller, lighter and more efficient
summary: The new model offers up to 375 Mbps speed and reduces energy consumption by almost 50% compared to the previous generation.

title: Intel manufactures its first high-volume chips with ASML's High NA EUV lithography
summary: The company announces that Panther Lake processors on the 18A node are qualified for the 0.55 NA machine.

title: Enthusiast creates physical cartridges for Steam games using SSDs
summary: Using 2.5-inch SSDs accompanied by an auto-start script, a user turns digital games into interchangeable cartridges.

title: Valve stops selling replacement parts for the Steam Deck LCD, complicating repairs
summary: The company removes replacement components for the LCD version from the market, making device maintenance difficult.

title: The solar eclipse on August 12 will offer a detailed duration map and a scientific opportunity for citizens
summary: The astronomical phenomenon will be visible throughout Spain and citizens will be able to participate in a citizen science experiment during the minute and 40 seconds of totality.

title: Vodafone and Orange rename their mobile networks 'TodoUnEquipo' for the World Cup final
summary: Both operators temporarily change the name of their networks to show support for the Spanish national team in the final.

title: Valve corrects course and will continue selling batteries for the Steam Deck LCD
summary: The company reverses its plan to remove batteries and confirms that iFixit will have stock next week.

title: SpaceX sets IPO price at $135 per share
summary: The space company values its initial public offering below expectations, amid anticipation for the Starship launch.

title: NTSB confirms that the driver of the crashed Tesla in Texas pressed the accelerator to the maximum
summary: The investigation concludes that the accelerator was pressed 100% at the time of the fatal crash, ruling out a mechanical failure.

title: OpenAI uses artificial intelligence to test its own AI and finds more bugs than humans
summary: The automated red teaming technique with AI proves to be more effective at detecting vulnerabilities in language models.

title: GPT-5.6 Sol solves a statistical conjecture that had remained unsolved for 30 years
summary: The AI model manages to refute a conjecture in 90 minutes, something mathematicians had not achieved in decades.

title: Apple Intelligence receives regulatory approval for launch in China with Alibaba technology
summary: Apple will integrate its artificial intelligence with Alibaba's Qwen model to meet Chinese market requirements.
